design_philosophy.core_loop: Real-time party-based combat with semi-autonomous AI companions, town exploration, character recruitment, and item crafting. Players control one character in battle while others follow pre-set strategies.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management via MP and SP allocation; character recruitment constraints create narrative branching and party composition decisions. Real-time combat positioning versus AI reliability.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a growing party of diverse recruits on an epic journey blending sci-fi space opera with fantasy adventure; discover hidden character stories through optional private interactions.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Pioneering real-time combat for SNES JRPGs with semi-autonomous party control rather than turn-based systems. Affection/Private Actions system emphasizes player choice affecting which companions join and their development arcs. Item creation crafting system allows outpacing shop progression. The game embraces asymmetric knowledge—not all 10 recruitable characters appear in a single playthrough, encouraging replays.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Party composition flexibility via character recruitment choices. Pre-battle strategy configuration for AI companions. Symbology (special techniques) cost MP, creating resource trade-offs. Item creation allows players to craft gear stronger than shop availability, enabling self-scaling difficulty.

progression.upgrade_system: Special Points (SP) earned from battles allocated to character abilities (cooking, item creation, etc). Equipment and weapon progression via shop purchases or crafted items. Symbology special techniques can be customized per character.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Character recruitment gated by story progression, party size thresholds, and specific story decisions. Some characters only recruitable if prerequisites met. Private Actions unlock additional character backstory and varying gameplay effects.
replayability_hooks: 10 recruitable characters available but only 8 slots per playthrough—encourages alt-runs to experience different party compositions
Private Actions reveal optional character-specific content
Story decision branching affects which characters become available
Multiple composition strategies for combat given AI autonomy

world_building_techniques: NPC dialogue in towns exploring plot and character backstories
Private Actions system—optional character-driven narrative moments triggered in towns
Voice acting in intro and battle voice clips (rare for SNES era)
Sci-fi framing device: contact with space-faring federation and galactic war context
Environmental exploration of towns and dungeons

faction_indicators: Space-faring federation vs. opposing galactic power (narrative backdrop)
Planetary allegiances implied through plot involving disease cure quest
danger_signals: New disease outbreak serving as story catalyst
Galactic war conflict affecting local planet
Time travel mechanics suggesting stakes manipulation
environmental_storytelling: Towns serve as hubs revealing local culture and NPC relationships
Character recruitment patterns suggest shifting alliances and trust
